discourse_trace: The southern revolt is coded as grievance discourse around rural marginalization, repression, and exclusion from the First Republic order.

Student-worker mobilization framed the crisis as a rejection of the established order and postcolonial dependence.; The provisional constitutional settlement framed military-led government as necessary to restore order and organize a new constitutional path.
settlement_response_to_trigger: 1972 student-worker uprising / First Republic rupture; 1972 military transition; Post-1972 sovereignty renegotiation

discourse_trace: The 1975 Socialist Charter and Constitution framed executive authority through revolutionary transformation, national sovereignty, and socialist refoundation.

Forces Vives mobilization framed legitimacy through democracy, free elections, and popular pressure against Ratsiraka’s regime.; The 1991 Convention transformed crisis claims into a negotiated transition, combining legality, consensus, and national reconciliation.; The transition convention and later constitutional integration used legal formalization to stabilize an extra-ordinary power-sharing arrangement.; The 1992 Constitution framed the exit from crisis as a restored constitutional order based on democratic institutions and popular sovereignty.

discourse_trace: The Zafy impeachment crisis is coded as a legality/accountability discourse around abuse of power and institutional control of the presidency.

Ravalomanana’s claim is coded as electoral mandate discourse: first-round victory, recount, and popular/electoral legitimacy.; Ratsiraka’s counter-claim is coded as constitutional/procedural legality discourse around the disputed election and runoff process.; Dakar/OAU mediation framed the crisis as requiring negotiated verification, stability, and avoidance of civil conflict.

discourse_trace: Rajoelina’s 2009 claim is coded as popular/revolutionary legitimacy against alleged authoritarianism and exclusion by Ravalomanana.; Ravalomanana and his supporters framed the 2009 transfer as a coup and defended constitutional continuity/electoral legality.; The one-day Military Directory is coded as an emergency/necessity transfer actor that converted coercive authority into transitional authority.; Ordinance n°2009-003 framed HAT authority through legal organization of public powers after the rupture.
The Maputo Charter framed transition through inclusiveness, power sharing, reconciliation, and internationally mediated return to order.
settlement_response_to_trigger: 2009 transition crisis; 2009 transition crisis / 2002 legacy; 2009-2011 transition crisis

discourse_trace: The SADC Roadmap framed legitimacy as return to constitutional order through negotiated, internationally monitored elections.
The exit-from-transition debate is coded around eligibility bargaining, exile/return, and acceptability of candidates to local/international actors.

The HCC justified a consensus prime minister and government as a legal-institutional response to political conflict over electoral laws.; The 2018 consensus government was framed as a stabilization device to preserve the electoral process and reduce conflict.

Opposition discourse in 2023 framed the election as illegitimate through citizenship, institutional-coup, and exclusion claims.; Rajoelina’s 2023 claim is coded as electoral mandate/legal continuity after reelection despite opposition contestation.; The HCC framed interim head-of-state functions as legal continuity during the electoral interim period.

Gen Z mobilization is coded as youth/popular legitimacy built around public-service grievances, corruption, poverty, and rejection of the incumbent order.; Randrianirina/CAPSAT authority is coded through military arbitration, order restoration, and claimed response to popular mobilization.; The HCC framed the 2025 rupture through vacancy/abandonment and formal recognition of a new authority to exercise Head-of-State functions.; Rajoelina-aligned counter-discourse is coded as anti-coup/legal continuity and rejection of military takeover after his departure.

The Refoundation Programme frames the transition through national refoundation, consultations, future constitutional redesign, and a managed electoral timetable.; International/regional discourse in 2026 emphasizes transition timing, return to elections, and stabilization under regional scrutiny.
